stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 15 observations with mean 30.7 % (within-factory SD = 8.7). factory B has 20 observations with mean 57.0 % (within-factory SD = 5.2). factory C has 5 observations with mean 42.4 % (within-factory SD = 7.8). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.2 and 18.0 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory C: 42.6 % (shrunk from raw 42.4). Range [42.5, 43.0] as between-factory SD varies from 7.2 to 18.0. Pooled mean: 45.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 45.3 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=12.0: B = 0.922.
Interval [42.5, 43.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (42.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (45.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
